text.title: spatial-ux-fundamentals Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns repeatedly observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Designer placed primary CTA at 4 m to 'give layout breathing room'; users cannot reliably tap.
antipattern.root-cause: Far field treated as cheap real estate for primary controls.
antipattern.fix: Move CTA to mid field (1.5-2.0 m). Lint rejects interactive controls outside near/mid bands.

antipattern.symptom: Mobile bottom-nav placed at chin region of HMD; neck fatigue after minutes.
antipattern.root-cause: 2D layout ported directly without zone remapping.
antipattern.fix: Reassign every element to a zone explicitly; chin-region forbidden for critical UI.

antipattern.symptom: Primary controls world-locked to room; user moves and cannot find them.
antipattern.fix: Anchor primary controls head-locked-with-decay or hand-attached.

antipattern.symptom: Users must remove headset to recover content placement; most-cited usability complaint.
antipattern.fix: Add recenter button + 'recenter' voice command; testable in spec.
